title: Online Tracing System
body_text:
Online Tracing System
1	Introduction
1.1	Overview of the Online Tracing System
The Online Tracing System (OLT) allows users to create trace requests for bill payments and view biller-initiated trace requests. Tracers and SuperTracers can create and view trace requests, but those created by Tracers require authorization from a SuperTracer.
To access OLT, Tracers and SuperTracers must log in to the Client Centre. After the individual is successfully authenticated, access to OLT is granted.
User management for the Client Centre is currently managed through CPS Admin. See the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on creating, modifying, and deleting Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers. Alternatively, if your financial institution is using User Management, see the User Management Part for procedures on adding and modifying Client Centre Users.
Note:	Bill payment trace requests may be subject to service charges. Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario can refer to the Payment Services Class A Fee Schedule, Form 2725.
If a trace canât be created on OLT, submit the trace request via ServiceNow. If ServiceNow is unavailable, complete the Electronic Bill Payment Trace Request, Form 1406, and forward it to Central 1. See the Electronic Bill Payments System Part for information on the following:
Responding to customer bill payment inquiries that donât require a trace request on OLT
Biller-initiated queries that canât be processed on OLT
Related Information

(available to Class A credit unions in BC and Ontario)
CPS Admin Application Part
Electronic Bill Payments System Part
1.2	OLT Availability
Tracers and SuperTracers can access OLT at any time. Central 1 responds to trace requests and enters biller-initiated traces on OLT as follows:
For financial institutions outside of Ontario: between 5:30 am and 4:30 pm PT, Monday to Friday.
For financial institutions in Ontario: between 8:30 am and 4:30 pm ET, Monday to Friday.
1.3	Billers that Donât Accept Trace Requests from Central 1
Select billers donât accept trace requests from Central 1. Go to the EBP Billers Listing (
https://billers.central1.com
) and perform a search to confirm if the select biller(s) doesnât accept trace requests from Central 1. If a trace request is needed for the select biller(s), advise the customer to contact the biller directly.
If an âNâ is displayed in the Traceable column of your search results, the biller doesnât accept trace requests from Central 1. However, if the Traceable column is blank, the biller accepts trace requests from Central 1. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: EBP Billers Listing Search Results
1.4	How OLT Corresponds to Bill Payments
The bill payment process begins when a customer makes a bill payment where the money is debited from the customerâs account and paid to the billerâs financial institution account.
If an error is made (e.g., paid to wrong biller, incorrect amount paid), the financial institution creates a trace request on OLT on behalf of the customer to request a refund or to make a correction. A variety of search criteria is available on OLT for creating a trace request. One method is to search on the trace number for a specific transaction. Trace numbers are provided on the Bill Payments Remitted (BPRE) report, which is available daily on the File and Report Exchange.
To reconcile traces, the financial institution generates a Closed â Settled report on OLT and reconciles the transactions to the postings on the Current Account Services (CAS) system.
